mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust labor market and expanding economy, which typically translates into strong consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a specific threshold, it indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with strong industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad-based economic strength across both consumer demand and manufacturing output. This synergy suggests healthy corporate revenues and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0 and INDPRO MoM growth > 0
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) during a late-cycle expansion signals a tight labor market, which can lead to accelerating wage inflation and pressure corporate profit margins, eventually leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.0% during a late-cycle expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and demand for related goods and services. This typically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where HOUST shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth above a critical threshold, erodes purchasing power and can trigger more aggressive monetary policy tightening, negatively impacting corporate earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) coupled with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer spending supported by accommodative monetary conditions. This combination is highly favorable for corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS shows positive MoM growth and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy expands.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO MoM growth exceeds 0.5% during a post-recession recovery.
